lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੁ ਬੀਚਾਰਿ ਮਾਰਿ ਮਨੁ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਤੁਮ ਸਾ ਮੀਤੁ ਨ ਅਵਰੁ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: aap beechaar maar man dekhiaa tum saa meet na avar koee |
lines.english: Reflecting upon my self, and conquering my mind, I have seen that there is no other friend like You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਤੂੰ ਰਾਖਹਿ ਤਿਵ ਹੀ ਰਹਣਾ ਦੁਖੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਦੇਵਹਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਸੋਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jiau toon raakheh tiv hee rahanaa dukh sukh deveh kareh soee |3|
lines.english: As You keep me, so do I live. You are the Giver of peace and pleasure. Whatever You do, comes to pass. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਨਸਾ ਦੋਊ ਬਿਨਾਸਤ ਤ੍ਰਿਹੁ ਗੁਣ ਆਸ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਭਈ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa manasaa doaoo binaasat trihu gun aas niraas bhee |
lines.english: Hope and desire have both been dispelled; I have renounced my longing for the three qualities.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਰੀਆਵਸਥਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਸੰਤ ਸਭਾ ਕੀ ਓਟ ਲਹੀ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: tureeaavasathaa guramukh paaeeai sant sabhaa kee ott lahee |4|
lines.english: The Gurmukh obtains the state of ecstasy, taking to the Shelter of the Saints' Congregation. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮਿ ਮਨੁ ਰਾਤਾ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਪਾਏ ਸਹਜ ਸੇਵਾ ॥੫॥੨੨॥
lines.translit: naanak raam naam man raataa guramat paae sehaj sevaa |5|22|
lines.english: O Nanak, one whose mind is imbued with the Lord's Name, finds the Guru's Teachings, and intuitively serves. ||5||22||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਗਲੇ ਸਭਿ ਜਪ ਤਪ ਜਿਸੁ ਹਰਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵਾ ॥
lines.translit: giaan dhiaan sagale sabh jap tap jis har hiradai alakh abhevaa |
lines.english: All wisdom and meditation, all chanting and penance, come to one whose heart is filled with the Invisible, Inscrutable Lord.
Aasaa, First Mehl, Panch-Padhay:
Your attachment to your family, your attachment to all your affairs
- renounce all your attachments, for they are all corrupt. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਰਿਦੇ ਰਵੈ ਸਰੀਰ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: saach naam ride ravai sareer |1| rahaau |
lines.english: and dwell upon the True Name within your heart and body. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹੁ ਅਰੁ ਭਰਮੁ ਤਜਹੁ ਤੁਮੑ ਬੀਰ ॥
lines.translit: mohu ar bharam tajahu tuma beer |
lines.english: Renounce your attachments and doubts, O brother,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਾ ਨਵ ਨਿਧਿ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: sach naam jaa nav nidh paaee |
lines.english: When one receives the nine treasures of the True Name,
his children do not weep, and his mother does not grieve. ||2||
Few are the Gurmukhs who swim across. ||3||
In this attachment, the world is drowning.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਦੀਖਿਆ ਲੇ ਜਪੁ ਤਪੁ ਕਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur deekhiaa le jap tap kamaeh |
lines.english: You have received the Guru's Teachings - now practice meditation and penance.
In this attachment, people are reincarnated over and over again.
Attached to emotional attachment, they go to the city of Death. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਤਾ ਏਹੁ ਮੋਹੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: nadar kare taa ehu mohu jaae |
lines.english: But if He bestows His Glance of Grace, then this attachment departs.
O Nanak, then one remains merged in the Lord. ||6||23||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਮੋਹੁ ਤੂਟੈ ਨਾ ਥਾਇ ਪਾਹਿ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: naa mohu toottai naa thaae paeh |5|
lines.english: If attachment is not broken, no one is approved. ||5||
He Himself does everything, the True, Invisible, Infinite Lord.
Aasaa, First Mehl:
I am a sinner, You are the Forgiver. ||1||
By Your Will, everything come to pass.
The intellect of the self-willed manmukh is engrossed in falsehood.
One who acts in stubborn-mindedness is ruined in the end. ||1||Pause||
Without the meditative remembrance of the Lord, it suffers in sin. ||2||
Renounce evil-mindedness, and you shall reap the rewards.
Whoever is born, comes through the Unknowable and Mysterious Lord. ||3||
Such is my Friend and Companion;
In all other transactions, one suffers loss.
meeting with the Guru, the Lord, devotion was implanted within me. ||4||
The Name of the Lord is pleasing to Nanak's mind. ||5||24||
Aasaa, First Mehl, Chau-Padhay:
Contemplate and reflect upon knowledge, and you will become a benefactor to others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਉ ਜਮੁ ਕਹਾ ਕਰੇ ਮੋ ਸਿਉ ਆਗੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tau jam kahaa kare mo siau aagai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: So what can the Messenger of Death do to me hereafter? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਪੰਚ ਰਾਸੀ ਤਾਂ ਤੀਰਥ ਵਾਸੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jaan panch raasee taan teerath vaasee |1|
lines.english: When you conquer the five passions, then you shall come to dwell at the sacred shrine of pilgrimage. ||1||
You shall hear the vibrations of the tinkling bells, when your mind is held steady.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਜਤੁ ਜੋਗੀ ਤਾਂ ਕਾਇਆ ਭੋਗੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jaan jat jogee taan kaaeaa bhogee |2|
lines.english: When the Yogi practices abstinence, then he enjoys his body. ||2||
When you abandon hope and desire, then you become a true Sannyaasi.
Through compassion, the naked hermit reflects upon his inner self.
Nanak does not know Your wondrous plays. ||4||25||
He slays his own self, instead of slaying others. ||3||
You, O Lord, are the One, but You have so many Forms.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਨ ਭਰੀਆ ਗੁਣ ਕਰਿ ਧੋਵਾ ॥
lines.translit: ek na bhareea gun kar dhovaa |
lines.english: I am not stained by only one sin, that could be washed clean by virtue.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰਾ ਸਹੁ ਜਾਗੈ ਹਉ ਨਿਸਿ ਭਰਿ ਸੋਵਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: meraa sahu jaagai hau nis bhar sovaa |1|
lines.english: My Husband Lord is awake, while I sleep through the entire night of my life. ||1||
Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹੁ ਜਾਗੈ ਹਉ ਨਿਸ ਭਰਿ ਸੋਵਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sahu jaagai hau nis bhar sovaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: My Husband Lord remains awake, while I sleep through the entire night of my life. ||1||Pause||
In this way, how can I become dear to my Husband Lord?
